title: The Future of Space Exploration
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Space exploration is one of the greatest (0) scientific endeavours of the modern age. Since the first satellites were launched in the 1950s, humans have made extraordinary (17) discoveries about our solar system and beyond. The journey to other planets is (18) fascinating to scientists and the general public alike. Recent advances in (19) technological equipment have made space missions cheaper and more reliable. Many people once thought it was (20) impossible to land on the moon, yet the event proved them wrong. The (21) achievement of sending humans into space required enormous courage and innovation. Plans for (22) exploration of Mars are already being developed by several international agencies. The photographs sent back from space probes are deeply (23) impressive. However, space programmes remain highly (24) expensive, raising questions about whether the funding could be better spent on Earth.

example.text: Space exploration is one of the greatest (0) SCIENTIFIC endeavours of the modern age. (SCIENCE)

questions.explanation: A plural noun is needed after 'extraordinary'; 'discover' becomes 'discovery', then the plural 'discoveries' by changing '-y' to '-ies'.
An adjective is needed after the verb 'is'; 'fascinate' becomes 'fascinating' by adding the suffix '-ing'.
An adjective is needed before 'equipment'; 'technology' becomes 'technological' by adding the suffix '-ical'.
A negative adjective is needed; the prefix 'im-' is added to 'possible' to mean 'not achievable'.
A noun is needed as the subject; 'achieve' becomes 'achievement' by adding the suffix '-ment'.
A noun is needed after 'Plans for'; 'explore' becomes 'exploration' by adding the suffix '-ation'.
An adjective is needed after 'deeply'; 'impress' becomes 'impressive' by adding the suffix '-ive'.
An adjective is needed after 'highly'; 'expense' becomes 'expensive' by adding the suffix '-ive'.
